title: Faraday Pattern — NONLINEAR_ESCAPE Failure Fixture
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates NONLINEAR_ESCAPE: amplitude driven too high; system transitions to disordered turbulent state. No stable pattern class reachable. QA analogue: state forced outside all orbit families — no legal return path.

pattern_basin_graph._note: Hysteresis prevents direct return: once in disordered regime, amplitude must be reduced to flat and re-ramped. The cert system records this as illegal (path is blocked without passing through flat).

obstruction_classes.witness: Turbulent regime at amplitude 0.99 exhibits broadband spatial spectrum; no dominant wavenumber. Hysteresis prevents direct return to hexagon basin without amplitude reset through flat.

validation_checks.description: Each step in witnessed_path is legal
validation_checks.actual: increase_amplitude_to_0.99: legal=true (transition to disordered is a real physical move)

validation_checks.description: return_in_k is true (can return to initial pattern in ≤k moves)
validation_checks.actual: return_in_k=false — hysteresis blocks direct return; must reset amplitude to flat first

validation_checks.description: current_class is a recognizable non-disordered pattern
validation_checks.expected: pattern class in {flat, stripes, squares, hexagons, oscillons, quasipattern}

witness.qa_analogue: State forced outside all orbit families. In QA terms: amplitude overdrive is equivalent to choosing a modulus where the state pair (b,e) has no finite orbit — not representable in the QA system.

witness.result: hysteretic_failure — system stays disordered until amplitude reset to flat basin
